dialogos.texto_en: Oh, big bro!
Uesugi-kun? You're alone?
I-it's not like I'm lonely or anything!\nI'm just doing what I want, you know!
I haven't said anything yet.
Ooh...
Aren't you with Yotsuba?
No, we're having free time and all that.\nI'm just wandering around as I like.
...What are you so restless about?
I see you're not with Nino or Miku.
I don't know where they went.\nI'm sure they're also checking out\nsouvenir shops or something.
I see.
What's up with those two?
I haven't the slightest idea.
...Are you hiding something, Itsuki?
I-I'm not hiding anything at all, Uesugi-kun?\nIt most definitely doesn't concern you.
Okay, then why don't you tell me what you know?
Oh, that's right!\nGuess what, big bro!
Huh?\nYeah, what is it?
Earlier, at the shop over there,\nI saw someone playing a 'sanshin' with Itsuki-san!
That was so amazing, wasn't it.
Y-yes!\nIt was just so wonderful, Raiha-chan!
Sanshin? Yeah, I know the sun shines nicely here.
No, no.\nA sanshin is a traditional Okinawan musical instrument.
Oh, I see.
I've actually heard that instrument's\nsound quite a bit since coming here.
You hear it a lot in various places, don't you.\nIt's an essential part of Okinawan music, after all.
They had a sanshin on display,\nand while we were watching,\nthe shopkeeper played it right in front of us.
Oh, that's a nice surprise.
Even without sheet music,\nshe was able to play it just by ear. That was so cool.
I heard they also have trial lessons\nfor tourists at that shop.
If I practiced,\ndo you think I could learn to play it too?
Who knows? With those trial lessons,\nyou might be able to play something nice.
And, even if you can't play it well,\njust producing some beautiful notes\ncould be really fun, I think.
Oh wow, I didn't know\nyou were interested in the sanshin, Itsuki.
Yeah.\nThe sound is simple and pure, and listening to it\ngives me some sort of healing feeling.
Can you play any instruments, Itsuki?
I have no experience,\nif I could play it well, I think it might be fun.
Unfortunately, we won't be able to participate\nin the trial lesson. It doesn't match our schedule.
Come to think of it, while the sanshin is a traditional\ninstrument, it seems various modifications\nhave been made to modernize its decoration.
Well, it's worth a try.\nIt's good to have an interest in something, isn't it?
Yes, if I hadn't come to Okinawa on this trip,\nI wouldn't have been able to experience the sound\nof a real sanshin like this.
Something might start off an encounter like this.
No one really knows what might spark\nfrom ANY encounter one has.
Maybe you'll become a sanshin player in the future,\nItsuki-san.
Haha, that would be pretty dang unexpected.
Hehehe,\nthat does seem like a fun future,\nbut I have my own dream that I want to fulfill.
Come to think of it, while the sanshin is a traditional\ninstrument, it seems various modifications\nhave been made to modernize its decoration.
It's a good opportunity.\nWe could learn a lot about the culture\nthrough the sanshin.
Yes, during this trip we'll be visiting\nvarious places in Okinawa, so I want to take\nit all in and enjoy everything we see.
That kind of thinking is just like you.
I'm not particularly good at history,\nbut I'm aiming to be a teacher,\nso I want to accumulate knowledge.
I think you too, Uesugi-kun,\nshould try to overcome your weaknesses\nas soon as possible, don't you think?
Huh? Me?
Yes. Please do your best.
Come to think of it, while the sanshin is a traditional\ninstrument, it seems various modifications\nhave been made to modernize its decoration.
Decorations, like those patterns.\nI don't know much about recent sanshin,\nbut I think the traditional pattern was snakeskin.
Indeed.\nIt seems they were made from the skin\nof the nishiki snake.
I thought it was a habu when I heard the word snake,\nbut now that I think about it,\na habu would be too small to use.
Oh yeah!\nI was pretty surprised, but apparently\nthe sound is different when real leather is used.
Yes. They said that a really clear sound comes out.
The sound changes depending on the skin.\nHuh, that's interesting.
For those who want to try playing one for the first\ntime, there are also ones that use artificial skin,\nso it might be good to buy and take home as a souvenir.
(She's casually talking about buying one,\n but a sanshin's definitely not cheap.\n She's making a subtle upper-class statement...)
